dimensions.note: Workflow-scope report with no AWS API access and no config schema. It only reads step artifacts already persisted by the upstream model; no tokens, passwords, or provider credentials are sourced, stored, or referenced.

All logging goes through the tryLog helper with structured placeholders (e.g. "Collected {clusters} cluster(s)...") and never string interpolation. Levels are appropriate: info on entry/exit, warn for skipped/unreadable artifacts and the degraded path. No secret or sensitive value is logged; only counts, spec names, handle names, and failed-field paths.

Never-throws posture is the design. Per-handle storage reads, JSON decode, and Zod safeParse failures are each narrowly caught, counted into skipped, and logged rather than aborting the run. An unexpected collection failure is absorbed by the outer try/catch in execute, which sets degraded=true and returns a still-valid payload. No writeResource exists (this is a report), so there is no partial-write risk.

Four test layers cover collect (happy path, unrelated model type, storage read failure, bad JSON, schema mismatch, unrelated spec name, all-optional-fields-omitted, no-matching-step), both comparators, renderMarkdown (empty state, non-zero skipped flag, summary counts, stable table order), and execute (sorted JSON rows, empty context, degraded path, and the negative guard asserting no csv/columns keys). Both success and failure paths are exercised with an in-memory dataRepository stub.

Pure read-and-shape with no side effects; running it repeatedly over the same step artifacts yields identical output. There is no create/delete lifecycle. Partial upstream data (missing or malformed artifacts) degrades to a smaller, still-valid report rather than failing.

The report makes no external HTTP or provider API calls and does no pagination. It consumes only the upstream model's persisted artifacts, which it validates field-by-field through hand-mirrored Zod schemas before use.
No network connections, file handles, or temporary files are opened. A single module-level TextDecoder is reused across decodes. There are no cloud resources to track or clean up.

README and manifest examples use generic placeholders (model name rds-fleet, workflow <workflow>, package names). RDS endpoint strings appear only in test fixtures as obviously synthetic values (e.g. prod-aurora.cluster-abc...rds.amazonaws.com) with no real account IDs, internal hostnames, IP literals, or CIDR/topology details. No real infrastructure identifiers are present in any published or shipped surface.
